M-054.NAME: mtproto-usage-telemetry
M-054.owns: PostgreSQL-backed MTProto usage events, IP-observation samples, connection sessions, traffic rollups, last-seen metadata, and abuse-signal records.
M-054.purpose: Persist metadata-only MTProto proxy usage so admins can analyze service activity without inspecting Telegram payloads.

exports.model-MTProtoUsageEvent: Append-only event row for handshake, close, bytes, error, unknown_sni, rejected_sni, active_connection, req_pq proof, and ip_observation events.
exports.model-MTProtoUsageSession: Per-assignment session row with start, end, duration, status, byte counters, and active flag.
exports.model-MTProtoUsageRollup: Hourly, daily, and monthly aggregate counters by assignment and global scope.
exports.model-MTProtoAbuseSignal: Observe-only abuse signal row for many IPs, high concurrency, traffic spikes, and repeated failures.
exports.fn-ingest-telemetry-batch: Store runtime telemetry batches idempotently by runtime event id while filtering configured trusted router-hop IP observations.
exports.fn-update-last-seen: Update last successful MTProto handshake timestamp per assignment.
exports.fn-is-trusted-proxy-hop: Detect configured RU/DE router hop addresses so infrastructure IPs do not become user-device evidence.

M-054.evidence: pass-local 2026-05-21: usage telemetry regression, encrypted IP handoff, RU SNI-router real-client-IP ingestion, trusted router-hop skip before persistence, IP_OBSERVATION no-inflation guards, 30-day raw event retention, scheduler maintenance, migration metadata guard, and Phase-43 backend wave passed.

data-contract.field-1: assignment_id links usage to MTProtoAssignment when SNI is known.
data-contract.field-2: Unknown or rejected SNI events may store masked SNI and safe reason codes but never derived secrets or tg links.
data-contract.field-3: Client IP is stored as HMAC/hash or prefix-based aggregate by default; Phase-43 permits encrypted raw IP only through M-061 admin-only observability with 90-day retention.
data-contract.field-4: Rollups must be sufficient for day, week, month, top users, last seen, connection count, duration, and traffic views.
data-contract.field-5: IP_OBSERVATION events update M-061 current/last IP evidence and abuse classifiers but must not inflate traffic, sessions, top-user, or timeseries usage counters.
data-contract.field-6: IP_OBSERVATION events whose client IP matches configured trusted router-hop addresses are skipped before M-054 row persistence and before M-061 encrypted raw-IP handoff.

constraints.constraint-1: Telemetry persistence must not block live MTProto proxy traffic.
constraints.constraint-2: Retention policy must keep raw events bounded while preserving rollups.
constraints.constraint-3: Backup/restore must preserve rollups and assignments without exposing secrets.
constraints.constraint-4: Phase-43 raw event retention is 30 days, while sessions and rollups must preserve the operator's three-month investigation window.
constraints.constraint-5: Infrastructure proxy-hop IPs must not appear as admin-visible Current IP, Last IP, IP history, or distinct user IP evidence.

M-054.hotspot: H-041: MTProto telemetry must support useful analytics without storing raw secrets, full proxy links, or unnecessary raw IP addresses
